message:
perf(connection): skip INTEGER PRIMARY KEY alias decode for non-alias SUM aggregates

Continuation of the retained-count-sum hot-path work in this branch.

`Connection::project_record_for_aggregate(...)` is called once per
row that the retained-autocommit overlay scan visits to feed
COUNT/SUM aggregates. It used to call
`parse_record_projected_column_offsets(payload, column_index,
rowid_alias_column_index)` which projects two slots: the column
under aggregate AND the INTEGER PRIMARY KEY alias slot, and then
ran a separate alias-validation step using the alias offset.

For an aggregate like `SUM(score)` on a table with
`id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, score INTEGER NOT NULL`, the alias slot
is irrelevant — the aggregate target is `score`. Decoding the
alias for every row inflates retained-aggregate scan cost
proportionally to row count.

Fix: pass `None` as the rowid-alias-column-index when projecting,
so only the actual aggregate column gets decoded. Keep the alias-
column validation, but only run it when the aggregate actually
targets the alias (`rowid_alias_column_index == Some(column_index)`).
That branch can use `projected.primary` directly because the
aggregate target IS the primary projected column in that case —
no need for the conditional `primary vs secondary` selector.

Net: removes one record-value decode per row from the SUM hot path
when the SUM target isn't the IPK alias, while preserving alias
validation for `SUM(id)` and equivalent.

New regression test
`test_retained_count_sum_scan_skips_unused_ipk_alias_decode`:
seeds two rows, runs `SELECT COUNT(*), SUM(score) FROM ...`
through the retained autocommit overlay, asserts
  - the result is correct (count=2, sum=30)
  - `retained_autocommit_overlay_hits >= 1` so we know we used
    the overlay scan
  - `record_decode.decoded_values.null_count == 0` — proves the
    alias slot (which is non-NULL but irrelevant) was NOT decoded
    on any row

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
